# Transcrição: Here's_why_you_HATE_small_talk

- **Fonte:** Here's_why_you_HATE_small_talk
- **Duração:** 01:10
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:10]

A lot of people don't like small talk, but they don't like small talk because the questions they're asking suck. Most people, when they do small talk, what are the questions they normally engage in? What do you do for a living? If you're asking questions like that, of course small talk is boring. My friends, if you are courageous enough to ask a set of different questions, that's going to allow you to engage in more engaging small talk. When I have a moment with someone, say I'm sitting on the plane or I'm engaging with someone in line, sometimes if I have the time and the moment allows it, I ask them things like, so tell me what's your favorite TV series? And if they say Game of Thrones or if they say Scrubs, then we're going to become best friends. But if they say Neighbors or Home and Away, then I will exit that conversation very quickly. I love asking this question too. If you had a full day off, what would you do with your time? What that does is these questions allow me to get a bigger insight into who these people are. I'm going to get a sense of the things they like to do, their hobbies. You'll get such a more rounded idea of who this person is, as opposed to just asking them what do they do. There's so much opportunity to practice in small talk and it's such a great way to improve our communication skills. Just start asking better questions and you'll find small talk not as excruciating.
